checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-012 concretely define profiles, invalid states, provenance requirements, task lifecycle boundaries, integrations, portability, migration behavior, and bypass resistance; AC-001 through AC-014 provide corresponding observable outcomes sufficient to prevent materially divergent product behavior.

The requirements, constraints, non-goals, and acceptance criteria consistently preserve predecessor ordering, prohibit fabricated provenance and bypasses, isolate lite profiles, and bound legacy migration; no directly conflicting goal or acceptance outcome is present.

AC-002, AC-004 through AC-008, and AC-014 cover registry errors, traversal and symlink escape, malformed/stale/forged provenance, the task-state boundary matrix, overbroad legacy exceptions, lite isolation, LF/CRLF parity, corrupt JSON, missing files, and unreadable inputs.

INV-001 through INV-008 identify the persisted-state gap, historical artifact generations, enforcement surfaces, parity obligations, missing retrospective record, and synchronized release baseline; the requirements explicitly resolve these findings through profiles, bounded migration records, global enforcement, and provenance rules.

REQ-004 requires valid Spec, Impl, and Task PASS before Approved or executable/completed task states, preserves Draft/Planned while awaiting human approval, and AC-005 verifies the full predecessor matrix including sudo and pre-existing state; constraints retain quality-gate-only authority for Done.

The specification defines observable policy for registry coverage, full/lite/legacy profiles, provenance, lifecycle coupling, enforcement integrations, migration, portability, regression coverage, and release synchronization, allowing implementation-policy review to select architecture without inventing product behavior.
